description_long: This is a historic-style public water fountain constructed primarily from weathered stone, featuring a central vertical column with an arched alcove and a decorative pediment at the top. The front face of the column houses a metal spout protruding above a small stone platform, with a metal grate below to catch water. The fountain is surrounded by a large, rounded stone basin with thick, curved walls, designed to collect and contain water. The basin sits atop a red-tiled platform, which is slightly raised and bordered, with some visible wear and chipping at the edges. Metal railings and a drainage grate are integrated into the basin on one side, likely for maintenance or overflow control. The overall appearance suggests durability and a design intended for frequent public use in urban parks or plazas.
description: A historic stone public water fountain with a central column, metal spout, and a large rounded basin set on a red-tiled platform. Features include a decorative pediment, metal grate, and integrated railings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched alcove, metal spout, and main access to the fountain, with the stone basin and red-tiled platform clearly visible.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back of the column, a metal railing, and the drainage grate within the basin, with less detail on the spout.
description_view_2: Opposite side view showing the plain back of the column and the full curve of the stone basin, with the tiled platform's edge visible.
description_view_3: Side view displaying the metal railing and drainage grate more prominently, as well as the thickness of the basin walls and the tiled base.
attribute_spout_type: straight metal pipe spout, horizontally oriented, protruding from the arched alcove
attribute_mounting_location: floor-standing, embedded in ground with a raised tiled platform
attribute_surrounding_infrastructure: red ceramic tiled platform, metal drainage grate, metal railing
